slides.text: 5 things you didn't know Claude could do
slides.sub: Everyone's searching 'what is Claude used for' right now. Here's the answer, with the exact prompts to steal.

slides.text: Reads a 100 page PDF in seconds
slides.sub: Drop in a contract, lease, or manual and ask: 'What are the 3 things I should be worried about in here?' It quotes the exact sections back to you. Works on school packets, work docs, terms nobody reads.

slides.text: Builds working apps from one sentence
slides.sub: Try: 'Build me a tip calculator with a dark theme.' It writes the code AND runs the app right in front of you. Calculators, habit trackers, mini games. no code knowledge needed.
Paste the ugly export and say: 'Clean this up, merge the duplicates, and tell me what you changed.' Then ask for totals, trends, or a chart. Minutes of work instead of your whole evening.
Paste 3 of your old emails, then: 'Learn my tone. Reply to this as me.' Drafts that actually sound like you. emails, DMs, reviews, cover letters. Nobody can tell.

slides.text: It can now use your browser FOR you
slides.sub: The new Claude for Chrome clicks buttons, fills forms, and compares pages while you watch. Price comparisons, research, signups. Full breakdown in the video on my page.
Follow @techyai_cj. one 'things you didn't know' drop every single week. Tomorrow: the full video version.
